qtype: Ratio and proportion, percentage profit, reverse percentage
tex:
Dinari sells fruit and vegetables.
\newline

(a) One day the mass of fruit and vegetables he sells is in the ratio fruit : vegetables $= 9 : 8$.
\newline

He sells $48\,\text{kg}$ of vegetables.
\newline

Find the mass of the fruit he sells.
\newline

(b) On another day he receives \$280 for the fruit and vegetables he sells.
\newline

The \$280 is in the ratio fruit : vegetables $= (c + 3) : (c - 1)$.
\newline

Find the amount he receives from selling the fruit.
\newline

(c) In one week Dinari buys fruit and vegetables for \$1620.
\newline

He sells the fruit and vegetables for \$1750.
\newline

Calculate his percentage profit.
\newline

(d) In another week Dinari sells fruit and vegetables for \$1738.
\newline

He makes a profit of 10\%.
\newline

Calculate the amount he paid for the fruit and vegetables in that week.
\newline

content.content:
One day the mass of fruit and vegetables he sells is in the ratio fruit : vegetables $= 9 : 8$.
\newline

He sells $48\,\text{kg}$ of vegetables.
\newline

Find the mass of the fruit he sells.
\newline

On another day he receives \$280 for the fruit and vegetables he sells.
\newline

The \$280 is in the ratio fruit : vegetables $= (c + 3) : (c - 1)$.
\newline

Find the amount he receives from selling the fruit.
\newline

In one week Dinari buys fruit and vegetables for \$1620.
\newline

He sells the fruit and vegetables for \$1750.
\newline

Calculate his percentage profit.
\newline

In another week Dinari sells fruit and vegetables for \$1738.
\newline

He makes a profit of 10\%.
\newline

Calculate the amount he paid for the fruit and vegetables in that week.
\newline

_rawBody:
Dinari sells fruit and vegetables.

\begin{parts}
\item One day the mass of fruit and vegetables he sells is in the ratio fruit : vegetables $= 9 : 8$.

He sells 48\,kg of vegetables.

\vgap

Find the mass of the fruit he sells.

\AnswerLine[][kg]
\Marks{2}

\item On another day he receives \$280 for the fruit and vegetables he sells.

The \$280 is in the ratio fruit : vegetables $= (c + 3) : (c - 1)$.

\vgap

Find the amount he receives from selling the fruit.

\AnswerLine[\$][]
\Marks{3}

\item In one week Dinari buys fruit and vegetables for \$1620.

He sells the fruit and vegetables for \$1750.

\vgap

Calculate his percentage profit.

\AnswerLine[][\%]
\Marks{2}

\item In another week Dinari sells fruit and vegetables for \$1738.

He makes a profit of 10\%.

\vgap

Calculate the amount he paid for the fruit and vegetables in that week.

\AnswerLine[\$][]
\Marks{2}

\end{parts}

tex:
(a) Ed invests \$500 in an account paying $r$\% per year **simple** interest.
\newline

At the end of 14 years the total amount in Ed's account is \$675.
\newline

Find the value of $r$.
\newline

(b) Eva invests \$400 at a rate of 2.2\% per year **compound** interest.
\newline

Calculate the total interest earned at the end of 11 years.
\newline

(c) Erin invests \$700 at a rate of $p$\% per **month** compound interest.
\newline

At the end of 21 years the value of Erin's investment is \$1074, correct to the nearest dollar.
\newline

Calculate the value of $p$.
\newline

content.content:
Ed invests \$500 in an account paying $r$\% per year \textbf{simple} interest.
\newline

At the end of 14 years the total amount in Ed's account is \$675.
\newline

Find the value of $r$.
\newline

Eva invests \$400 at a rate of 2.2\% per year \textbf{compound} interest.
\newline

Calculate the total interest earned at the end of 11 years.
\newline

Erin invests \$700 at a rate of $p$\% per \textbf{month} compound interest.
\newline

At the end of 21 years the value of Erin's investment is \$1074, correct to the nearest dollar.
\newline

Calculate the value of $p$.
\newline

_rawBody:
\begin{parts}
\item Ed invests \$500 in an account paying $r$\% per year \textbf{simple} interest.

At the end of 14 years the total amount in Ed's account is \$675.

\vgap

Find the value of $r$.

\AnswerLine[$r$ = ][]
\Marks{3}

\item Eva invests \$400 at a rate of 2.2\% per year \textbf{compound} interest.

\vgap

Calculate the total interest earned at the end of 11 years.

\AnswerLine[\$][]
\Marks{3}

\item Erin invests \$700 at a rate of $p$\% per \textbf{month} compound interest.

At the end of 21 years the value of Erin's investment is \$1074, correct to the nearest dollar.

\vgap

Calculate the value of $p$.

\AnswerLine[$p$ = ][]
\Marks{3}

\end{parts}

tex:
(a) A box contains 50 cuboids.
\newline

Each cuboid has a mass of $135\,\text{g}$.
\newline

The total mass of the cuboids and the box is $7\,\text{kg}$.
\newline

Calculate the mass of the box.
\newline

Give your answer in grams.
\newline

(b) A solid cube of side $4\,\text{cm}$ is fixed to the base inside an empty cube of side $6\,\text{cm}$.
\newline

Water is poured into the larger cube until it reaches the top of the smaller cube.
\newline

Calculate the amount of water poured into the larger cube.
\newline

(c) 

The diagram shows a solid triangular prism of length $20\,\text{cm}$.
\newline

The cross-section is an equilateral triangle with side length $4\,\text{cm}$.
\newline

The prism is made of wood with a density of $0.85\,\text{g}$/cm$^3$.
\newline

Calculate the mass of the prism.
\newline

[Density = mass $\div$ volume]

(d) 

The diagram shows a solid cone with base radius $10\,\text{cm}$ and height $24\,\text{cm}$.
\newline

(i) (e) Show that the **total** surface area of the cone is $1131\,\text{cm}^2$, correct to the nearest cm$^2$.
\newline

[The curved surface area of a cone with base radius $r$ and slant height $l$ is $A = \pi r l$.
\newline
]

(f) The total surface area of the cone is painted.
\newline

\begin{subsubparts}

(g) The cost to paint the cone is \$1.71.
\newline

Calculate the cost to paint $1\,\text{cm}^2$ of the cone.
\newline

Give your answer in cents.
\newline

(h) One tin of paint has enough paint to cover $2.5\,\text{m}^2$.
\newline

Calculate the number of these cones that can be painted completely using one tin of paint.
\newline

\end{subsubparts}

\newline

Water is poured into the larger cube until it reaches the top of the smaller cube.
\newline

Calculate the amount of water poured into the larger cube.
\newline

The diagram shows a solid triangular prism of length $20\,\text{cm}$.
\newline

\newline

The prism is made of wood with a density of $0.85\,\text{g}$/cm$^3$.
\newline

Calculate the mass of the prism.
\newline

[Density = mass $\div$ volume]

The diagram shows a solid cone with base radius $10\,\text{cm}$ and height $24\,\text{cm}$.
\newline

Show that the \textbf{total} surface area of the cone is $1131\,\text{cm}^2$, correct to the nearest cm$^2$.
\newline

[The curved surface area of a cone with base radius $r$ and slant height $l$ is $A = \pi r l$.
\newline
]

The total surface area of the cone is painted.
\newline

Calculate the cost to paint 1\,cm$^2$ of the cone.

Give your answer in cents.
One tin of paint has enough paint to cover 2.5\,m$^2$.
Calculate the number of these cones that can be painted completely using one tin of paint.

_rawBody:
\begin{parts}
\item A box contains 50 cuboids.

Each cuboid has a mass of 135\,g.

The total mass of the cuboids and the box is 7\,kg.

\vgap

Calculate the mass of the box.

Give your answer in grams.

\AnswerLine[][g]
\Marks{2}

\item A solid cube of side 4\,cm is fixed to the base inside an empty cube of side 6\,cm.

Water is poured into the larger cube until it reaches the top of the smaller cube.

\vgap

Calculate the amount of water poured into the larger cube.

\AnswerLine[][cm$^3$]
\Marks{2}

\item \,

\relinput{Figures/Q04c-TriangularPrism.tex}

\vgap

The diagram shows a solid triangular prism of length 20\,cm.

The cross-section is an equilateral triangle with side length 4\,cm.

The prism is made of wood with a density of 0.85\,g/cm$^3$.

\vgap

Calculate the mass of the prism.

[Density = mass $\div$ volume]

\AnswerLine[][g]
\Marks{4}

\item \,

\relinput{Figures/Q04d-Cone.tex}

\vgap

The diagram shows a solid cone with base radius 10\,cm and height 24\,cm.

\begin{subparts}
\item Show that the \textbf{total} surface area of the cone is 1131\,cm$^2$, correct to the nearest cm$^2$.

[The curved surface area of a cone with base radius $r$ and slant height $l$ is $A = \pi r l$.]

\Answerspace[3cm]
\Marks{4}

\item The total surface area of the cone is painted.

\begin{subsubparts}
\item The cost to paint the cone is \$1.71.

\vgap

Calculate the cost to paint 1\,cm$^2$ of the cone.

Give your answer in cents.

\AnswerLine[][cents]
\Marks{1}

\item One tin of paint has enough paint to cover 2.5\,m$^2$.

\vgap

Calculate the number of these cones that can be painted completely using one tin of paint.

\AnswerLine[][]
\Marks{2}

\end{subsubparts}

\end{subparts}

\end{parts}

qtype: Speed, distance, time calculations and unit conversions
tex:
(a) Naomi runs $100\,\text{m}$ in 15 seconds.
\newline

Calculate Naomi's average speed in kilometres per hour.
\newline

(b) Olav runs for 45 minutes at a speed of $9.5\,\text{km/h}$.
\newline

He then runs $8.1\,\text{km}$ at a speed of $7.5\,\text{km/h}$.
\newline

Calculate Olav's average speed for the whole run.
\newline

(c) A train has length $p$ metres.
\newline

The train passes through a station of length $q$ metres.
\newline

The speed of the train is $v$ kilometres per hour.
\newline

Find an expression for the time the train takes to completely pass through the station.
\newline

Give your answer in seconds, in terms of $p$, $q$ and $v$.
\newline

content.content:
Naomi runs $100\,\text{m}$ in 15 seconds.
\newline

Calculate Naomi's average speed in kilometres per hour.
\newline

Olav runs for 45 minutes at a speed of $9.5\,\text{km/h}$.
\newline

He then runs $8.1\,\text{km}$ at a speed of $7.5\,\text{km/h}$.
\newline

Calculate Olav's average speed for the whole run.
\newline

A train has length $p$ metres.
\newline

The train passes through a station of length $q$ metres.
\newline

The speed of the train is $v$ kilometres per hour.
\newline

Find an expression for the time the train takes to completely pass through the station.
\newline

Give your answer in seconds, in terms of $p$, $q$ and $v$.
\newline

_rawBody:
\begin{parts}
\item Naomi runs 100\,m in 15 seconds.

\vgap

Calculate Naomi's average speed in kilometres per hour.

\AnswerLine[][km/h]
\Marks{2}

\item Olav runs for 45 minutes at a speed of 9.5\,km/h.

He then runs 8.1\,km at a speed of 7.5\,km/h.

\vgap

Calculate Olav's average speed for the whole run.

\AnswerLine[][km/h]
\Marks{3}

\item A train has length $p$ metres.

The train passes through a station of length $q$ metres.

The speed of the train is $v$ kilometres per hour.

\vgap

Find an expression for the time the train takes to completely pass through the station.

Give your answer in seconds, in terms of $p$, $q$ and $v$.

\AnswerLine[][s]
\Marks{3}

\end{parts}

tex:
A bag contains 5 white balls and 3 black balls.
\newline

(a) 

(i) (b) Marwan picks a ball from the bag at random and then replaces it.
\newline

Find the probability that the ball is white.
\newline

(c) Naomi picks a ball from the bag at random and then replaces it.
\newline

She repeats this 120 times.
\newline

Find the number of times the ball is expected to be white.
\newline

(d) Oscar picks a ball from the bag at random.
\newline

He replaces it and then picks a second ball from the bag at random.
\newline

(i) (e) Find the probability that the balls are the same colour.
\newline

(f) Find the probability that the balls are **not** the same colour.
\newline

(g) Priya picks 3 of the 8 balls from the bag at random without replacement.
\newline

Find the probability that she picks two white balls and one black ball.
\newline

\newline

\newline

\newline

\newline

\newline

Find the probability that the balls are \textbf{not} the same colour.
\newline

Priya picks 3 of the 8 balls from the bag at random without replacement.
\newline

Find the probability that she picks two white balls and one black ball.
\newline

A bag contains 5 white balls and 3 black balls.

 (  ,  )

qtype: functions, inverse functions, composite functions, algebraic fractions, index equations
tex:
$$f(x) = 2x + 5 \qquad g(x) = 1 - 2x \qquad h(x) = \dfrac{1}{x + 1},$\,$ x \neq -1 \qquad j(x) = 2^x$$

(a) Find $\text{g}(-3)$.
\newline

(b) Find   $\text{f}(x)\text{g}(x) + \text{fg}(x) + 1$.
\newline

Give your answer in its simplest form.
\newline

(c) Find $\text{g}^{-1}(x)$.
\newline

(d) Find $\text{hh}(1)$.
\newline

(e) Simplify   $\dfrac{1}{\text{f}(x)} - \text{h}(x)$.
\newline

Give your answer as a single fraction in its simplest form.
\newline

(f) Find $x$ when   $\text{j}(x) = \dfrac{1}{32}$.
\newline

(g) Find $x$ when   $\text{j}^{-1}(x) = 0$.
\newline

\newline

\newline

\newline

\newline

\newline
